message:
test(readme): cover set_axis + DataFrame.copy (fd90.251 / br-3662n)

Extends readme_element_wise_operations_compiles_and_runs to cover 3
axis/clone methods that previously had no integration coverage:

- Series.set_axis(Vec<IndexLabel>) — replace the row index labels
- DataFrame.set_axis(labels, axis) — replace row (axis=0) or column
  (axis=1) labels
- DataFrame.copy() — deep clone (Series.copy was already tested)

Verified: 36 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Include missing values so the harness proves bucket-local extrema, non-null counting, and grouped rolling index formatting.","notes":"cc-pandas triage 2026-04-22: live pandas oracle for DataFrameGroupBy.rolling min|max|count landed in commit 3771fd1; generic fixture dispatch for the same operations wired in today's commit f7a4e59. live_oracle_dataframe_groupby_rolling_{min,max,count}_matches_pandas pass under rch.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":60,"created_at":"2026-04-22T08:03:09.816257297Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:35:12.591656642Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:35:12.588773849Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","testing"]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-33pap","title":"fd90.214: Cover squeeze/iat/at/lookup in Introspection test","description":"fd90.214: Cover squeeze/iat/at/lookup in readme_dataframe_introspection.

The README's DataFrame Introspection section (lines 1168-1178) documents 4 APIs that aren't yet in the integration test:

- DataFrame.squeeze(axis) — collapse single-column/single-row DataFrame → Series
- Series.squeeze() — collapse single-cell Series → Scalar (returns Result<Scalar, Box<Series>>)
- Series.iat(0) — by-position scalar access
- Series.at(&label) — by-label scalar access
- DataFrame.lookup(&row_labels, &col_names) — multi-position lookup

Scope:
- Extend readme_dataframe_introspection_compiles_and_runs to call each.

+{"id":"frankenpandas-3662n","title":"fd90.251: Cover DataFrame.set_axis/copy + Series.set_axis","description":"fd90.251: Cover DataFrame.set_axis / DataFrame.copy / Series.set_axis.

Three index/axis manipulation methods uncovered:
- Series.set_axis(labels: Vec<IndexLabel>) (line 6109) — replace the row index labels
- DataFrame.set_axis(labels, axis) (line 20234) — replace row OR column labels
- DataFrame.copy() (line 16196) — deep clone

Scope:
- Extend an existing test with all 3 calls.

All 36 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-36qc","title":"[HIGH] ci: no cargo-audit / cargo-deny in CI — supply-chain CVEs in arrow/calamine/rusqlite deps go undetected","description":"Supply-chain security audit of .github/workflows/ci.yml + workspace deps.

**Observation.**
- Workspace pulls arrow 58, parquet 58, calamine 0.34, rusqlite 0.39, bumpalo 3.20, rust_xlsxwriter 0.94, serde 1.0.228, thiserror 2.0.18, regex 1.12, chrono 0.4, and ~dozens of transitive deps (see Cargo.lock 58 KB).
- ci.yml has format, check, clippy, workspace test, conformance test, bench, phase2c-gate, governance-gate. It does NOT run:
    cargo audit                  — security advisories against crates.io / rustsec DB
    cargo deny check advisories  — same, plus license + version-ban policies
    cargo outdated               — alerts on unbumped deps
- `cargo audit` is not even installed locally (exit: "no such command: audit").
- No deny.toml exists in the repo.
- No dependabot / renovate config in .github/ (only ci.yml, no dependabot.yml).

**Consequence.** A published CVE against arrow, calamine, rusqlite, or any transitive dep lands without notice. The lib-updater workflow runs on demand (see UPGRADE_LOG.md) rather than on a schedule; between sweeps the workspace can be exposed for weeks. The 2024 supply-chain-attack pattern (backdoored npm deps, etc.) has no defense here for Rust equivalents — the RustSec Advisory DB would catch them via cargo-audit.

**Severity.** HIGH — public-API library surface + IO parsers that consume untrusted bytes (CSV/JSON/Parquet/Excel/Feather) + SQL IO means any parser CVE in a dep is directly reachable from library callers.

**Fix shape:**
1. CI job `security`: runs `cargo install --locked cargo-audit` (cached), then `cargo audit --deny warnings`. Fails CI on any advisory.
2. CI job `licenses`: adds deny.toml with allowed licenses (MIT, Apache-2.0, BSD-3-Clause, ISC, MPL-2.0, Unicode-3.0), then `cargo deny check licenses advisories bans sources`. Fails CI on any policy violation.
3. `.github/dependabot.yml` for weekly deps refresh — groups by ecosystem so PRs are tractable.
4. Optional: `cargo update --dry-run -Zunstable-options --locked` on nightly as a drift canary.

Blast radius: ci.yml (+1 security job ~25 lines), deny.toml (+1 file ~40 lines), dependabot.yml (+1 file ~30 lines). No source code changes.

Parallel consideration: br-frankenpandas-8irq (pandas version pin) is a related supply-chain concern on the Python side.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T19:27:10.669216155Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-22T22:00:36.533961671Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T22:00:36.532026671Z","close_reason":"Added CI supply-chain audit jobs, deny.toml policy, weekly dependabot updates, and conformance guards","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","review","security","supply-chain"]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-378p","title":"fp-frame Series.unique ordering preservation parity","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T10:05:41.442669679Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T10:13:11.978105656Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T10:13:11.976246339Z","close_reason":"Series.unique now retains a single null marker per pandas semantics, first-seen ordering preserved, commit 7806847, 2 new tests + 1 migrated","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-37uy","title":"fd90.52: SqlReflectedTable::indexes_for_column / unique_constraints_for_column","description":"Add symmetry to SqlReflectedTable accessors. fd90.51 added foreign_keys_for_column(col) but not the parallel indexes_for_column(col) and unique_constraints_for_column(col). Each returns a Vec of entries whose columns Vec contains the named column (so multi-column indexes match if the column appears anywhere in the column list). Useful for answering is this column indexed and is this column part of a unique constraint queries. Pure ergonomic accessors. Tests cover: column appearing as first/middle/last position in a multi-column index, column not in any index, multiple matches.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"cc-pandas-2","created_at":"2026-04-26T04:02:11.413141879Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T04:05:13.694017547Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T04:05:13.690432130Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["api","fd90","review","sql"]}
